samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal. Active low, defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal. Active low, defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal. Triggered to initiate write operations.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal. Triggered to initiate read operations.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input. Configurable width representing the data to be written into the FIFO.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output. Configurable width representing the data read from the FIFO.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="8">Configurable width of the FIFO data bus.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="16">Configurable depth of the FIFO memory.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Dual-port RAM instantiation
        <instance id="ram" module="dual_port_RAM" width="WIDTH" depth="DEPTH">
            <port id="wclk" connection="wclk"/>
            <port id="wenc" connection="wen"/>
            <port id="waddr" connection="waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="wdata" connection="wdata"/>
            <port id="rclk" connection="rclk"/>
            <port id="renc" connection="ren"/>
            <port id="raddr" connection="raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="rdata" connection="rdata"/>
        </instance>
        
        // Write pointer management
        <reg id="waddr_bin" width="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <always_comb>
                if (winc && wrstn) begin
                    waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
                end else if (!wrstn) begin
                    waddr_bin <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <wire id="wptr" width="4">
            {waddr_bin[3], waddr_bin[3]^waddr_bin[2], waddr_bin[2]^waddr_bin[1], waddr_bin[1]^waddr_bin[0]}
        </wire>
        <reg id="wptr_buff" width="4">
            <always_comb>
                if (winc && wrstn) begin
                    wptr_buff <= wptr;
                end else if (!wrstn) begin
                    wptr_buff <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <assign wen = winc && wrstn;>
        // Read pointer management
        <reg id="raddr_bin" width="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <always_comb>
                if (rinc && rrstn) begin
                    raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
                end else if (!rrstn) begin
                    raddr_bin <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <wire id="rptr" width="4">
            {raddr_bin[3], raddr_bin[3]^raddr_bin[2], raddr_bin[2]^raddr_bin[1], raddr_bin[1]^raddr_bin[0]}
        </wire>
        <reg id="rptr_buff" width="4">
            <always_comb>
                if (rinc && rrstn) begin
                    rptr_buff <= rptr;
                end else if (!rrstn) begin
                    rptr_buff <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <assign ren = rinc && rrstn;>
        // Full/Empty signal generation
        <assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_buff[3], rptr_buff[3]^rptr_buff[2], rptr_buff[2]^rptr_buff[1], rptr_buff[1]^rptr_buff[0]});>
        <assign rempty = (rptr == wptr);>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Asynchronously manages read and write operations using dual-port RAM and pointer buffers to track full/empty status.</implementation>
</module>
